item.title: All You Need to Know About VoIP
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
All You Need to Know About VoIP

What is VoIP? How does it work? How is it different from analog telephone systems? If you’re looking for answers to these questions, then you are in the right place. This article will discuss the concept of VoIP, what it offers and how is it different from other commonly-used telephone systems.

What Is VoIP?

VoIP stands for Voice over Internet Protocol, which is a type of phone service that operates over the internet. It allows you to make calls, without the need of a telephone network. Instead, you need to have an internet connection to communicate via VoIP. Most people prefer using VoIP over traditional analog phone service because of the fact that it is a way cheaper option and allows people to save their hard-earned money.

How Does VoIP Work?

There are two types of signals, analog and digital. Analog signals are those which are continuous with time i.e. their amplitude varies continuously with time. On the other hand, the amplitude of discrete signals swings between two fixed levels that are 0 (low) and 1 (high). A digital signal comprises of a combination of 0s and 1s. Voice signals are classified as analog signals because it varies continuously with time. If we connect a microphone to an oscilloscope and check how our voice signal looks like, we will find a type of sinusoidal curve which is continuous and has numerous amplitude levels. When we talk on the phone, the microphone detects our voice as an analog signal and then the telephone system either converts it into a digital signal or processes it as it is. You must be familiar with the concept that data travels in the form of packets over the internet. VoIP takes our voice as the input signal and converts it into a digital signal which travels over the internet and reaches the phone of the person on the other end. The analog voice signal is first converted into a digital signal which travels in the form of an IP packet. Then, the digital signal is converted back into an analog signal, which the person on the other end receives. VoIP does not need the traditional circuit-switched PSTN network, rather it is based over the internet and hence, requires less resources. Some people believe VoIP is a means of making free voice calls, since you don’t have to pay the hefty charges your local telephone company would ask you for, which makes VoIP so cheap that it almost looks like it’s a free service.

How Can You Make a VoIP Call?

There are two ways you can make a VoIP call.
1.    IP Phones
Another way of using VoIP service is to use an IP phone which is a user-equipment specially designed for making phone calls over the internet. It has an in-built analog-to-digital converter, which directly connects to your router via a RJ-45 ethernet cable. Traditional analog handsets use an RJ-11 connector instead of the RJ-45 ethernet cable. The description might make you think that IP phones look different than the traditional analog phones, but that’s not true. They look exactly the same on the outside but definitely have different internal circuitry.
2.    Computer-to-Computer Calls
Some people don’t like the idea of using handheld phone because it requires them to hold the phone with one hand for a long period of time, which might make them feel tired or make them feel a bit outdated. If you’re one of the people who don’t feel comfortable in using a handheld telephone, instead you want to stick to something that’s modern and handsfree, then there’s good news for you. There are a couple of software which allow you to make VoIP calls through your computer. All you need is a stable internet connection, a microphone, and a speaker or a set of headphones to make the call. With this method, you don’t have to worry about paying huge sums of money every month in the name of telephone bills. VoIP calls will cost you very less as compared to your local telephone connection, no matter which part of the world you’re trying to reach.

How Is VoIP Different from Analog Telephones?

Let’s see how VoIP is different from analog telephones. First, let’s talk about the way signals travel in each of these calling services. Analog telephones send their signals over copper wires, which need to be rented, hence cost more as compared to VoIP. As mentioned earlier, VoIP sends its signals in the form of packets, which does not require special infrastructure; hence it costs low. A typical landline service provider will ask you for 50-100 dollars per month for a single line rental, whereas for a VoIP connection you’d need to pay around $25 per month, which is very cheap as compared to the traditional phone service. Therefore, most businesses prefer VoIP over analog telephones since it helps them save money that they would have otherwise invested in renting telephone lines. If we talk about the features, VoIP offers much more features aside calling over the internet. As mentioned earlier, you can reject & redirect calls, and also enjoy the feature of conference calling via a VoIP connection. These features are not available in the traditional analog telephone system. They are a mere way of sending and receiving calls. A great feature that makes VoIP much better than analog phones is voicemail transcription. It is a service that allows you to convert your audio voicemail into text through a specialized software. The converted voicemail messages are then sent as text to your email address with the caller’s details such as their name and number, and the exact time of call. You can then read the voicemail whenever you get time. Voicemail transcription saves you from listening to a series of audio messages left by callers one after the other. It often becomes annoying to listen to so many audio messages at the same time and then remember what each person was saying. With voicemail transcription, you will have an individual written record for every caller, which you can refer to anytime, to see what they said. Voicemail transcription is a solution which every business needs nowadays, since written message are reliable and easy to deal with as compared to audio messages. You can enjoy this feature by getting a VoIP connection installed. Another advantage of using VoIP instead of analog phones is location flexibility. With the traditional landline phone, it was impossible for you to travel and take the phone along with you. Same is the case for businesses. Many companies could not expand their business due to location constraints. VoIP is a savior for such companies since it has the benefit of relocating anywhere, anytime. All you need is a stable internet connection and you can setup your VoIP system very easily. Moreover, adding or removing lines in a VoIP system is much easier as compared to the traditional analog phone where you need to rent out another line and install the required hardware, by calling a technician. Doing the same with a VoIP connection is much easier and you don’t even need a technician for it. If we talk about the developments in technology, IP telephony is evolving day by day, leaving the traditional phones way behind in the race. Majority of the people are parting ways with their analog phone connections and moving towards VoIP because of its numerous benefits. In the near future, traditional phones would surely become obsolete and everyone will shift towards VoIP for making calls without breaking the bank and staying connected with the world.

What Is On-Site PBX?

PBX (Private Branch Exchange) is a type of phone system which is used inside organizations and companies as their personal telephone network. The employees of the organization can make calls within their premises and also communicate with the outside world through PSTN (Public Switched Telephone Network). Through a PBX, a company can install as many telephones as they like within their premises, without the need of worrying about extra charges. On the contrary, if PBX was not an option, they would have to install separate trunk lines for every telephone that they need inside the organization, which would increase the installation cost. PBX can handle different types of calls including analog, digital, VoIP, and SIP, and also has the ability to convert these calls into PSTN format, when communicating with the outside world. The PBX system can be located within the organization or on the cloud. On-site PBX refers to the system installed within the organization while UC (Unified Communication) solution or UCaaS (Unified Communication as a Service), refers to a cloud based PBX system. On-site PBX is better than analog phone systems because of the fact that they are cheaper and do not require technicians or engineers when adding or removing a new line.

On-Site PBX vs. VoIP

PBX is different from VoIP because the telephones within the organization and even outside are connected through copper wires. Which means when a person calls someone over a PBX connection, it means they are physically connected. However, VoIP calls are not physically connected, instead they are connected over the internet, where voice signals are encapsulated into IP packets. If we talk about the cost, VoIP is much cheaper than PBX because it does not need physical wiring for installation. All it needs is a stable internet connection and an IP phone. New connections are easy to make with VoIP as compared to PBX. With a PBX connection, you won’t be able to enjoy HD voice calling since it is based on copper wires. When signals travel through a copper wire, they are opposed with external as well as internal noise. Electrons present in copper wires generate an intrinsic noise which affects the quality of your call and degrades the voice signals sent over it. This affects the caller’s experience and may lead you towards losing sales. VoIP offers noise-free, crystal-clear calling experience where you simply do not have to worry about the quality of your audio. HD voice calling is the best solution when it comes to dealing with noise and voice quality degradation.

Is VoIP a Good Choice?

Certainly, yes! VoIP is the best choice when it comes to choosing a telephone option to meet your calling needs. IP telephony is the way of the future, and after some years you will notice a prominent decline in the use of traditional analog phones and on-site PBX systems. This is because of the countless advantages and cost-effectiveness that VoIP offers over the other two options. If you are planning to install a telephone system in your organization, then you should surely opt for VoIP. People around you might tell you not to go for it because not many people use this system but as the world is moving towards digitalization, you will give yourself a pat on the back when you realize how beneficial it is. Ten years down the lane, almost everyone will shift towards VoIP, hence taking this step right now would be an intelligent move because later on, it might cost you a bit more because of the huge increase in its demand. Voice over IP systems are definitely a good choice and you should go ahead and install one in your organization. We provide the best VoIP solutions for small and medium businesses. If you think it’s time for your business to shift towards VoIP, then please don’t hesitate to contact us. We have a proven track record of providing VoIP solutions for many companies, and our clients have always loved our service.

item.title: All You Need to Know About the Features of VoIP
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP) has gained quite a lot of popularity in recent times because of the countless benefits it offers. It has allowed people to get rid of the traditional phone system and shift to something with better features. VoIP will enable you to talk through phone calls over the internet. If you're someone who wants to know what features you can enjoy with a VoIP connection, then this article is what you need to read right now. It will walk you through the various features that this technology has to offer so that it gets easy for you to get rid of the useless telephone line connection, which does nothing except consuming a chunk of your bank balance. Here are the features of VoIP that make it more convenient than the traditional telephone line system.

Mobile and Desktop App

As a business owner, all you are looking for is easy solutions, and VoIP offers you a great solution, i.e., mobile and desktop apps. You don't necessarily need to use a telephone device for VoIP phone calls. You can call others through your mobile phone and desktop, which is a great blessing for small businesses.

Low Cost

One of the best features of VoIP, as compared to other services, is that you don't need to spend a considerable amount on calling people. With VoIP, you can save a lot of money since businesses need to communicate with their clients daily.

Multiple Offices in One System

If you have offices in multiple locations and are using a telephone line, you would install a separate line for each office. On the other hand, VoIP allows you to connect various offices based on different locations under the same VoIP connection. You don't need to invest money in buying numerous links.

HD Voice

As a business, you need to communicate with others efficiently, and the best way to do so is to have a crystal-clear voice being sent out from your side. The person on the other end would be impressed by the HD call quality, and they wouldn't face any difficulty while listening to what you're saying.

Voicemail Features

Voicemail is another excellent feature that helps you communicate with the customers when you are not available to talk, but the voicemail feature in VoIP is different from the traditional voicemail option present in regular telephone lines.

Voicemail on Email

Another great voicemail feature that VoIP offers is sending the voicemail message to your email ID, saving you from the hassle of listening to those messages on your telephone device. If you are out of your office for some reason, you can always check your email on your smartphone to read the transcribed messages that people have left in your voicemail.

Call Recording

As a small business owner, you would love to record phone calls so that you can refer to them later on. Sometimes, it is challenging to remember little details such as names and numbers that someone told you during a phone call, and that's when the VoIP call recording feature comes in handy. It allows you to listen to your phone call as many times as you want. Moreover, you may also want to record calls for security reasons since people tend to discuss confidential details through phone calls. With a call recording option, you can keep an eye on what information your employees send out through calls.

CRM Integration

Customer Relationship Management (CRM) integration is an excellent idea for small businesses because it saves time and helps your business grow in multiple ways. For instance, when a customer calls you, the CRM tool will record all of their details, including their name, number, time of call, call duration, and in some cases, call recording as well. It also acts as a great marketing tool since it studies and analyses customer behavior and suggests what you should do to keep your customers happy. With CRM integration and acceptable business practices, your business will likely flourish and grow to become a significant player in the market.

Increased Mobility

One of the significant benefits of using VoIP instead of the traditional telephone lines is that you can move around and access the VoIP connection, as long as you are connected to the internet. On the other hand, telephone devices had the restriction of staying at a particular location only since they were not connected to the internet.

Customizable Music on Hold

Putting a call on hold means that you will make the other person wait for some time. Doing so can negatively affect your business since your customer might get bored waiting for you and instantly end the call, making you lose the chance of earning profit. With VoIP, you have the option of setting the music of your choice as the hold tune to decrease the chances of the person to end the call.

Auto Attendant

As a business owner, you want your customers to have a great experience when they reach you via a phone call. A great way to improve caller satisfaction is to use the auto attendant feature that VoIP systems offer. It allows you to set up a menu that will direct your caller to the relevant department they want to have a word with. A virtual attendant will answer the call, welcome the caller and connect them to the right person. The auto-attendant feature leaves the caller satisfied and creates an excellent impression of your company.

Conference Calls

As a business owner, you likely need to include multiple people on the same phone call. This feature is not available in the traditional telephone lines, but you can host a conference call with VoIP. Be it an internal office meeting or a conference call, including multiple clients, VoIP will help you.

After all

The features VoIP offers are great for small and medium businesses since they allow the company to operate in the best possible way. Using a VoIP connection will keep you from missing important calls and leaving your customers dissatisfied, and it will allow you to gain profits in the long run.

item.title: VoIP Phone Systems: The Future of Business Communications
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
VoIP Phone Systems: The Future of Business Communications

In today's fast-paced business world, having a reliable and efficient communication system is crucial for any organization. Traditional phone systems are becoming increasingly outdated and expensive, which is why more and more businesses are turning to Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP) phone systems. In this article, we will discuss the benefits of VoIP business phone systems and how they can help your organization.

What is VoIP?

VoIP is a communication technology that allows you to make and receive phone calls over the Internet instead of a traditional phone line. VoIP phone systems convert your voice into digital signals that can be transmitted over the internet, making it possible to communicate with anyone, anywhere in the world. The quality of VoIP calls is often better than traditional phone lines, and it's often more affordable.

Benefits of VoIP for Businesses
  1. Cost Savings
One of the biggest advantages of VoIP is cost savings. VoIP systems are often much more affordable than traditional phone systems. Because VoIP uses the internet to transmit calls, there are no long-distance charges, and the cost of hardware is often lower. Many VoIP providers offer flexible pricing plans that can be customized to meet the specific needs of your business.
  1. Flexibility and Scalability
VoIP systems are highly flexible and can be easily scaled to meet the needs of your business. You can add or remove lines and features as your business grows and changes. VoIP systems also allow you to make and receive calls from anywhere in the world, as long as you have an internet connection.
  1. Advanced Features
VoIP systems offer a range of advanced features that are not available with traditional phone systems. Some of the most popular features include call forwarding, voicemail to email transcription, auto-attendant, conference calling, and call recording. These features can help increase productivity, streamline communication, and improve customer service.
  1. Integration with Other Business Tools
Many VoIP providers offer integration with other business tools, such as customer relationship management (CRM) software and email. This integration can help streamline communication and increase productivity.
  1. Reliability
VoIP systems are highly reliable and offer excellent call quality. Because VoIP calls are transmitted over the internet, there is no risk of physical line damage, which can cause disruptions with traditional phone systems. Additionally, many VoIP providers offer redundancy and backup systems to ensure that your communication system is always up and running.

Conclusion

VoIP business phone systems are an excellent choice for any organization looking to improve their communication system. VoIP offers cost savings, flexibility, scalability, advanced features, integration with other business tools, and reliability. If you're considering upgrading your organization's communication system, VoIP is a great option to consider.

item.title: VoIP: Enabling Remote Work From Anywhere in the Modern World
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
VoIP: Enabling Remote Work From Anywhere in the Modern World The rise of remote work in the modern world has made it essential for businesses to have communication systems that allow their employees to work from anywhere. This is where Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP) comes in, providing businesses with the ability to communicate and collaborate from anywhere in the world. What is VoIP? VoIP is a technology that allows voice communication over the internet instead of traditional phone lines. This means that instead of using copper wires to transmit voice signals, VoIP uses digital packets that travel over the internet. One of the major advantages of VoIP is that it can be used with any device that has an internet connection, including computers, tablets, and smartphones. This makes it easy for employees to work remotely from anywhere in the world, without the need for traditional office phone systems. Benefits of VoIP for Remote Work
  1. Mobility
VoIP allows employees to work from anywhere in the world, as long as they have an internet connection. This means that they can stay connected to the office, clients, and colleagues, no matter where they are.
  1. Cost Savings
VoIP can save businesses money on their communication costs. Traditional phone systems typically charge per minute or per call, while VoIP providers usually offer unlimited calling plans for a flat monthly fee. This can be especially helpful for businesses with remote employees who make frequent long-distance calls.
  1. Advanced Features
VoIP offers a range of advanced features that can improve communication and collaboration within a business. These features include call forwarding, voicemail to email transcription, video conferencing, and more. These features make it easy for remote workers to collaborate with colleagues and stay connected to the office.
  1. Scalability
VoIP is easily scalable, making it easy for businesses to add or remove lines as needed. This is particularly helpful for growing businesses or those that experience fluctuations in call volume.
  1. Integration with Other Business Systems
VoIP can integrate with other business systems, such as customer relationship management (CRM) software. This can streamline communication and improve customer service, even when employees are working remotely. How to Choose a VoIP Provider for Remote Work When choosing a VoIP provider for remote work, there are several factors to consider, including:
  1. Mobility
Ensure that the VoIP provider offers a mobile app that allows employees to make and receive calls from anywhere in the world.
  1. Features
Choose a provider that offers the features that are most important for remote work, such as call forwarding, voicemail to email transcription, and video conferencing.
  1. Reliability
Choose a provider with a good track record of reliability and uptime to ensure that your business communications are always available.
  1. Customer Support
Look for a provider that offers good customer support, including technical support and assistance with setup and configuration. In Conclusion VoIP is a powerful tool that enables remote work from anywhere in the world. With its mobility, cost savings, advanced features, scalability, and integration with other business systems, VoIP is an essential communication system for modern businesses with remote workers. When choosing a VoIP provider, consider mobility, features, reliability, and customer support to find the best solution for your business.

item.title: The Benefits of VoIP for Small Businesses
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
In today's fast-paced business environment, effective communication is crucial for success. Small businesses, in particular, need to leverage every advantage they can to stay competitive. Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP) technology offers a powerful solution for modern communication needs. At Nemerald, we understand the unique challenges faced by small businesses, and we're here to show you how VoIP can transform your operations. In this post, we'll explore the key benefits of VoIP for small businesses, including cost savings, improved communication, and scalability.

1. Cost Savings

One of the most significant advantages of VoIP for small businesses is the potential for cost savings. Traditional phone systems can be expensive to install and maintain, often requiring significant upfront investment in hardware and ongoing costs for line rentals and call charges. VoIP, on the other hand, uses your existing internet connection to make and receive calls, reducing or even eliminating many of these expenses.

Lower Call Costs

VoIP services typically offer lower rates for both local and international calls compared to traditional phone services. Many VoIP providers, including Nemerald, offer unlimited calling plans that can drastically reduce your monthly phone bill.

Reduced Hardware Costs

With VoIP, there's no need for expensive PBX (Private Branch Exchange) systems. All you need is a reliable internet connection and VoIP-compatible devices, such as IP phones or even your existing smartphones and computers. This can result in significant savings on equipment and maintenance.

Minimal Setup Fees

Setting up a VoIP system is generally quicker and more affordable than installing traditional phone lines. This is particularly beneficial for small businesses that may not have the budget for extensive telecommunications infrastructure.

2. Improved Communication

VoIP offers a range of features that enhance communication within your business and with your customers. These features go beyond simple voice calls, providing a comprehensive communication solution.

Advanced Features

VoIP systems come with a variety of advanced features, such as voicemail-to-email, call forwarding, auto-attendant, and conference calling. These tools can help streamline your communication processes and ensure that you never miss an important call.

Flexibility and Mobility

VoIP allows employees to make and receive calls from anywhere with an internet connection. This is ideal for remote work scenarios or businesses with multiple locations. Employees can use their VoIP service on their smartphones, laptops, or tablets, ensuring they're always connected, regardless of their physical location.

Enhanced Collaboration

With integrated features like video conferencing and instant messaging, VoIP fosters better collaboration among team members. These tools can help improve productivity and make it easier to coordinate projects and meetings, whether your team is in the office or working remotely.

3. Scalability

As your business grows, your communication needs will evolve. VoIP systems are inherently scalable, making it easy to adjust your services to match your business requirements.

Easy to Add or Remove Lines

Adding new phone lines with a traditional phone system can be a complex and costly process. With VoIP, you can easily add or remove lines as needed, without the need for additional hardware or complicated installations. This flexibility is perfect for growing businesses or those with fluctuating staffing levels.

Customizable Plans

VoIP providers offer a range of service plans that can be tailored to your specific needs. Whether you're a small startup with a handful of employees or a growing company with multiple departments, you can find a VoIP plan that fits your requirements and budget.

Future-Proof Technology

VoIP technology is continually evolving, with new features and improvements being added regularly. By adopting VoIP, your business can stay ahead of the curve and take advantage of the latest advancements in communication technology.

Conclusion

VoIP technology offers numerous benefits for small businesses, from cost savings and improved communication to scalability and flexibility. At Nemerald, we're committed to helping small businesses thrive by providing reliable, high-quality VoIP services tailored to your needs. If you're ready to take your business communication to the next level, contact us today to learn more about how VoIP can benefit your small business. --- Nemerald VoIP: Connecting Businesses, Empowering Success

item.title: Privacy Policy
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES LLC (“NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES”) is committed to protecting the privacy of customers and users of NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ website, www.nemerald.com, NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ Services, and any other NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES offering. By purchasing, subscribing to, or using NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ services or website, Customer agrees to the terms and conditions set forth in this Policy. For other terms and conditions that govern Customer’s use of NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ services and its website, please review NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ Terms and Conditions.

INFORMATION NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES COLLECTS. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES shall collect personally identifiable information, including but not limited to name, physical address, email address, telephone number, credit card information, and other personal information (collectively, “PII”), from Customers when initiating service and in connection with the provision or marketing of products and services. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES may collect PII from Visitors in connection with any visit to NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ Websites, any communication about NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ services and products, or other transactions or interactions with NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES.

NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES may also collect non-personally identifiable information from Customers and Visitors regarding usage of NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ services or NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ Website (“Non-PII”). Non-PII may include Website pages viewed, time spent using certain services, demographic data, cookies, and other information which does not specifically identify any one individual.

“Cookies” are pieces of information that may be placed on Customer’s computer by a Website for the purpose of facilitating and enhancing Customer’s communication and interaction with that Website. Many Websites use cookies for these purposes. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES may use cookies to customize Customer’s visit to NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ Websites and for other purposes to make Customer’s visit more convenient or to enable NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES to enhance NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ service.

“Cookies” are pieces of information that may be placed on Customer’s computer by a Website for the purpose of facilitating and enhancing Customer’s communication and interaction with that Website. Many Websites use cookies for these purposes. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES may use cookies to customize Customer’s visit to NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ Websites and for other purposes to make Customer’s visit more convenient or to enable NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES to enhance NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ service.

Customer may stop or restrict the placement of cookies on Customer’s computer, including third party cookies, or flush them from Customer’s browser by adjusting Customer’s Web browser preferences, in which case Customer may still use NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ Website, but it may interfere with some of its functionality.

USE OF PERSONALLY IDENTIFIABLE INFORMATION. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ Internal Use: In general, NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES uses personally identifiable information to serve NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ existing and prospective customers and to enhance and extend NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ customer relationships. By understanding how Customer uses NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ services and NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ website, NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES is able to customize and personalize NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ interactions with you. More specifically:
NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES uses personally identifiable information for billing purposes, to provide services or complete transactions Customer has requested, to anticipate and resolve problems with Customer’s services, and to create and inform Customer of products or services from NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES or others that better meet Customer’s needs.

Subject to Customer’s agreement, NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES uses e-mail, telemarketing, and direct mail to inform Customer about NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES products or services NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES thinks will interest you. If Customer does not wish to receive these types of communications Customer can choose at any time not to continue receiving them by contacting NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES at info@nemerald.com

NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES uses mobile device information to conduct anonymous mobile analytics so NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES has a better idea on how Customer interacts with Customer’s mobile device.

To allow Customer to capture and upload Customer’s profile picture, the application needs access to the camera. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES does not use this information for any purposes except for the designed use as mentioned here.

To allow Customer to select a picture from Customer’s photo gallery and upload it to use the fax service and/or update Customer’s profile picture. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES does not use this information for any purposes except for the designed use as mentioned here.

NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES sends push notifications from time to time for Customer to get call alerts.

While Customer may choose not to receive marketing information from us, Customer will continue to receive bills, customer service-related notifications, and other similar information from us electronically or otherwise. Customer will also continue to receive communications directly related to each service Customer purchases or subscribes to.

NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ Customer Surveys: Periodically, Customer may be able to participate in NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ surveys to help NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES improve its services. Any personally identifiable information that NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES may collect from Customer in a survey will ONLY be used internally by NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES. And unless otherwise explained in the instructions for a particular customer survey, any personally identifiable information Customer provides to NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES for purposes of that survey will be used in accordance with this Policy.

DISCLOSURE OF PERSONALLY IDENTIFIABLE INFORMATION. Because information about NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ existing and prospective customers is one of NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ most important business assets, NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES seeks to protect it and keep it confidential. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES does not share personally identifiable information with unaffiliated third parties, nor does NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES sell it to third parties, unless permitted to do so in accordance with this Policy.

Customer’s Consent: NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES may disclose Customer’s personally identifiable information and sensitive information if Customer consents to the disclosure in advance. Depending on the service, NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES may obtain Customer’s consent in a number of ways, including:

  • In writing;
  • Verbally;
  • Online, by clicking on a link or button; or
  • Through the use of a dialing string or buttons on a telephone handset.

In general, NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES may release personally identifiable information without Customer’s consent when NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES believes release is appropriate to do one of the following:

  • Comply with the law or a lawful government request (e.g., lawful subpoena or court order);
  • Enforce or apply NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ agreements to sell or provide services to Customer or enforce compliance with website policies;
  • Bill and collect for services; or
  • Protect or defend NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ legal rights and property, the interests of customers and website users, and the rights of third parties (e.g., to address allegations about fraudulent or unlawful activity on a NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES account);
  • Protect health and safety of users or general public in an emergency.

Disclosure to Affiliates: NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES may share Customer’s personal data between NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES and business affiliates where it is necessary to support the services provided to you.

In order to verify payment methods and prevent fraud, NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES verifies personally identifiable information, including payment information, with payment providers and fraud prevention services. If at any time Customer applies for a credit line with NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES, NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES may perform a credit inquiry with credit reporting agencies, and such inquiry may be noted on Customer’s credit record. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES may use automated decision-making systems to verify payment methods, assess creditworthiness, or prevent fraud, and NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES may contact Customer requesting additional information when needed.

Disclosure to Unaffiliated Third Parties: NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES may share personally identifiable information with unaffiliated third parties only as necessary to complete a transaction or perform a service that Customer has requested, or to support the technical operation and maintenance of NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ services and of NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ website. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES may also share personally identifiable information with or to NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ agents, outside vendors or service providers to perform functions on NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ behalf. For example, NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES uses third party service providers to perform functions such as analyzing data, providing marketing assistance, providing customer service and processing orders. When the unaffiliated third-party acts solely on NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ behalf, NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES requires that party to follow NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ privacy practices. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES does not sell its customer lists or any personally identifiable information associated with its customers to third parties.

Business Transfers: Information about NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ users and customers, including personally identifiable information, may be disclosed as part of any merger, acquisition, or sale of company assets. It may also be disclosed in the unlikely event of an insolvency, bankruptcy, or receivership in which personally identifiable information would be transferred as one of the business assets of NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES.

Emergency Calls: If Customer dials for emergency services (e.g., 911 in the U.S.), NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES may provide personally identifiable location information to a public safety answering point; emergency medical service provider or dispatch provider; public safety, fire service, or law enforcement official; hospital emergency or other medical care facility; or to a service provider that provides such information to such emergency responders.

CUSTOMER PROPRIETARY NETWORK INFORMATION (VoIP Customers). In the normal course of providing telecommunications and interconnected voice-over-Internet-protocol (“interconnected VoIP”) services to its users and customers, NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES collects and maintains certain customer proprietary network information (“CPNI”). Customer’s CPNI includes the types of telecommunications and interconnected VoIP services Customer currently purchase or subscribe to, how Customer uses those services (for example, Customer’s calling records), and billing information related to those services. Customer’s NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES telephone number, name, and address do not constitute CPNI.

NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES would like to share Customer’s CPNI within NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ company and with NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ affiliates and authorized agents in order to offer Customer additional products and services that may fit Customer’s needs. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES does not sell, trade, or otherwise share Customer’s CPNI with anyone outside of NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES or with anyone who is not authorized to represent NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES to offer its services or to perform functions on NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ behalf, except as the law may require or Customer may authorize.

Protecting Customer’s CPNI is important to us. In the United States, Federal law permits NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES to use this information to provide the telecommunications and interconnected VoIP services Customer purchases or subscribes to, and to bill and collect for those services. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES may also use or disclose Customer’s CPNI for legal or regulatory reasons such as to respond to a court order, to investigate fraud, to protect NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ rights or property, to protect against the unlawful use of NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ services, or to protect other users. Customer has a right and NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES has a duty under federal law to protect the confidentiality of Customer’s CPNI.

In order to opt-out and restrict access to Customer’s CPNI for NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ own marketing purposes, please contact NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES within 30 days of this notice by emailing info@nemerald.com. Restricting NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ use of Customer’s CPNI will not affect NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ provision of any service or product to which Customer currently subscribes, nor will Customer necessarily eliminate all types of NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES marketing.

SECURITY AND DATA INTEGRITY. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES has invested and deployed a wide variety of technology and security features to make reasonable efforts to ensure the privacy of information on its network. In addition, NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES has implemented operations guidelines to ensure Customer and Visitor privacy is safeguarded throughout its organization. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES will continue to revise policies and implement additional security features as new technologies become available. However, no system or service can give a 100% guaranty of security, especially a service that relies upon the public Internet. Therefore, Customer acknowledges the risk that third parties may gain unauthorized access to Customer’s information when using or accessing NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ services and NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ network.

NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ USE OF BANNER ADS. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES may use third-party advertising companies to place advertisements about NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES products and services on other websites. These advertising companies may use cookies, web beacons, or other technologies to measure the effectiveness of NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ ads. The advertising companies may use anonymous information about Customer’s visits to NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ and other websites in order to measure advertising effectiveness and offer selective ad content. But they use an anonymous identifier, such as a number, to identify you. They do not link to or collect any personally identifying information. The use of such cookies, web beacons, and other technologies is subject to the advertising companies’ privacy policies, not the privacy policy of NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES does not control and is not responsible for how third-party sites handle personal information of users.

SOCIAL MEDIA FEATURES. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ website includes Social Media Features, such as the Facebook or Twitter button. These Features may collect Customer’s IP address, which page Customer is visiting on NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ site, and may set a cookie to enable the Feature to function properly. Social Media Features are either hosted by a third party or hosted directly on NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ Site. Customer’s interactions with these Features are governed by the privacy policy of the third-party company providing them. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES does not control and is not responsible for how third party social media sites handle personal information of users.

REFERRAL PROGRAMS. If Customer choose to use NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ referral programs to refer someone to NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ site, NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES will ask Customer for the intended recipient’s name, email address, and phone number. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES will store and use this information to send emails inviting him or her to visit the site. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES stores this information for the sole purpose of sending the emails and tracking the success of NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ referral program. By agreeing to the terms in this privacy policy Customer acknowledges that Customer has received any referral recipient’s consent to provide his or her information to NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES. Customer’s referral may contact NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES at info@nemerald.com to request that NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES removes this information from NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ database.

BLOGS AND FORUMS. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ website offers publicly accessible blogs or community forums. Customer should be aware that any information Customer provides in these areas is public and may be read, collected, and used by others who access them and may remain on the public forum indefinitely. To request removal of Customer’s personal information from NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ blog or community forum, contact NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES at info@nemerald.com. In some cases, NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES may not be able to remove Customer’s personal information, in which case NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES will let Customer know if NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES is unable to do so and why. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES is not liable for the distribution of personal information posted on a public forum. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES encourages all users to exercise caution when providing personal information in blogs and community forums.

TESTIMONIALS. With Customer’s input and consent, NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES may post a customer testimonial by Customer on NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ website, which may include Customer’s name, industry, company, title, or other identifying information. If Customer wants Customer’s testimonial removed or wish to give or withdraw consent please contact NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES at info@nemerald.com.

FEEDBACK. From time to time, NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES may send Customer surveys, comment cards, customer satisfaction forms, or other requests to provide feedback. Customer hereby grants NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES, its licensors, and suppliers a perpetual, unlimited, worldwide fully-paid up, royalty free license to use all feedback, answers, ideas, comments, or other information Customer provides to NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES.

ENFORCEMENT. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES regularly reviews its compliance with this Privacy Policy. Please contact NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES with any questions or concerns regarding this Privacy Policy or NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ treatment of personal information. When NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES receives formal written complaints, it is NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ policy to contact the complaining customer or user regarding his or her concerns.

UPDATING OF NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ POLICY. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES will revise or update this Policy if NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ practices change or as NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES changes existing services, add new services, or develop better ways to inform Customer of services NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES thinks will interest you. If NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES makes any material changes, NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES will notify Customer by email (sent to the e-mail address specified in Customer’s account) or by means of a notice on this website prior to the change becoming effective. Customer should refer back to this page for the latest information and the effective date of any changes.

But they use an anonymous identifier, such as a number, to identify you. They do not link to or collect any personally identifying information. The use of such cookies, web beacons, and other technologies is subject to the advertising companies’ privacy policies, not the privacy policy of NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES does not control and is not responsible for how third-party sites handle personal information of users. SOCIAL MEDIA FEATURES. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ website includes Social Media Features, such as the Facebook or Twitter button. These Features may collect Customer’s IP address, which page Customer is visiting on NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ site, and may set a cookie to enable the Feature to function properly. Social Media Features are either hosted by a third party or hosted directly on NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ Site. Customer’s interactions with these Features are governed by the privacy policy of the third-party company providing them. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES does not control and is not responsible for how third party social media sites handle personal information of users. REFERRAL PROGRAMS. If Customer choose to use NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ referral programs to refer someone to NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ site, NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES will ask Customer for the intended recipient’s name, email address, and phone number. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES will store and use this information to send emails inviting him or her to visit the site. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES stores this information for the sole purpose of sending the emails and tracking the success of NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ referral program. By agreeing to the terms in this privacy policy Customer acknowledges that Customer has received any referral recipient’s consent to provide his or her information to NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES. Customer’s referral may contact NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES at info@nemerald.com to request that NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES removes this information from NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ database. BLOGS AND FORUMS. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ website offers publicly accessible blogs or community forums. Customer should be aware that any information Customer provides in these areas is public and may be read, collected, and used by others who access them and may remain on the public forum indefinitely. To request removal of Customer’s personal information from NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ blog or community forum, contact NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES at info@nemerald.com. In some cases, NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES may not be able to remove Customer’s personal information, in which case NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES will let Customer know if NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES is unable to do so and why. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES is not liable for the distribution of personal information posted on a public forum. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES encourages all users to exercise caution when providing personal information in blogs and community forums. TESTIMONIALS. With Customer’s input and consent, NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES may post a customer testimonial by Customer on NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ website, which may include Customer’s name, industry, company, title, or other identifying information. If Customer wants Customer’s testimonial removed or wish to give or withdraw consent please contact NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES at info@nemerald.com. FEEDBACK. From time to time, NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES may send Customer surveys, comment cards, customer satisfaction forms, or other requests to provide feedback. Customer hereby grants NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES, its licensors, and suppliers a perpetual, unlimited, worldwide fully-paid up, royalty free license to use all feedback, answers, ideas, comments, or other information Customer provides to NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES. ENFORCEMENT. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES regularly reviews its compliance with this Privacy Policy. Please contact NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES with any questions or concerns regarding this Privacy Policy or NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ treatment of personal information. When NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES receives formal written complaints, it is NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ policy to contact the complaining customer or user regarding his or her concerns. UPDATING OF NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ POLICY. NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES will revise or update this Policy if NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES’ practices change or as NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES changes existing services, add new services, or develop better ways to inform Customer of services NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES thinks will interest you. If NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES makes any material changes, NEMERALD TECHNOLOGIES will notify Customer by email (sent to the e-mail address specified in Customer’s account) or by means of a notice on this website prior to the change becoming effective. Customer should refer back to this page for the latest information and the effective date of any changes.
A business phone system with unlimited capabilities for reliable communication between teams and clients.

item.title: How to transfer calls using the Polycom VVX 400 Series phone

item.content:encoded: Two methods used for powering up VoIP phones are demonstrated, as well as the process behind connecting your VoIP phone to the internet.

item.title: Accessibility
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
nemerald Accessibility Statement

Updated: December 2026.

General nemerald strives to ensure that its services are accessible to people with disabilities. nemerald has invested a significant amount of resources to help ensure that its website is made easier to use and more accessible for people with disabilities, with the strong belief that every person has the right to live with dignity, equality, comfort and independence.

Accessibility on nemerald makes available the UserWay Website Accessibility Widget that is powered by a dedicated accessibility server. The software allows nemerald to improve its compliance with the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG 2.1).

Enabling the Accessibility MenuThe nemerald accessibility menu can be enabled by clicking the accessibility menu icon that appears on the corner of the page. After triggering the accessibility menu, please wait a moment for the accessibility menu to load in its entirety.

Disclaimernemerald continues its efforts to constantly improve the accessibility of its site and services in the belief that it is our collective moral obligation to allow seamless, accessible and unhindered use also for those of us with disabilities.

In an ongoing effort to continually improve and remediate accessibility issues, we also regularly scan nemerald with UserWay's Accessibility Scanner to identify and fix every possible accessibility barrier on our site. Despite our efforts to make all pages and content on nemerald fully accessible, some content may not have yet been fully adapted to the strictest accessibility standards. This may be a result of not having found or identified the most appropriate technological solution.

Here For YouIf you are experiencing difficulty with any content on nemerald or require assistance with any part of our site, please contact us during normal business hours as detailed below and we will be happy to assist.

Contact UsIf you wish to report an accessibility issue, have any questions or need assistance, please contact nemerald Customer Support as follows:

Email: info@nemerald.com
